segments.text: hey folks welcome to this video on
python 3.12 and FST strings I'm your
host nafal Islam a developer Advocate at
sonar this story starts with pep 701 now
pep 701 set out to fix a lot of the
things and F strings that we ran into in
the previous versions of python here you
have an FST string with the dictionary
bag and we're trying to retrieve the
item wand now this causes a syntax error
because you're not allowed to have a
single quote within an F string that
starts with a single quote now you might
want to solve this by backs slashing
that single quote but guess what that's
illegal inside of an F string at this
point in time you might be a little
surprised with all of this well not to
fear because in Python 3.12 we solve
this problem with the new and enhanced
fstring parsing engine so you can see
here that before wand from bag it
doesn't work but here letters works just
fine and you can do a lot more with
strings so for example you can do what
you used to do which is to evaluate
expressions within F strings but you can
also have FST strings within FST strings
however you can't really have FST
strings beside FST strings you should be
using FST strings within F strings but
also notice how wonderfully the new
syntax ER tells us exactly where our
problem is within our F string so what
changed let's dive into it the first
thing that changed is the grammar itself
so in Python 3.11 a string or strings
are just a bunch of strings put together
now that's not exactly the most
intuitive way of understanding what a
string is in Python 3.12 however you're
going to see that the grammar has been
expanded much further and it still
includes our favorite string as being
more strings so let's dive into what
this exactly means using the
tokenizer the first thing that we're
going to do is import a package called
tokenize and then import a package
called IO so tokenize is going to take a
bunch of bytes and turn it into a bunch
of tokens what that effectively means is
that we're going to take our F string
and then break it down into its
components and once we do break it down
into its components you're going to see
that python 3.12 and 3.11 parse strings
very differently so we have our string
we're going to pass it into io. bytes
iio because we want a by stream that we
can pass into tokenize do tokenize and
of course we're going to encode the
string in utf8 once we've encoded the
string what we're going to do is pass it
into token eyes. tokenize and of course
we're going to use by stream. readline
and we're not going to invoke it because
tokenize do tokenize expects a
callable and finally we're going to
print out the list of token tokens that
we generate now this comparison would
not be complete if I didn't do the exact
same thing in Python 3.11 so that's what
I'm going to do on the right hand side
you are going to see python 311 311 has
a lot fewer tokens that are generated
and in 312 you generate a lot more
tokens the reason for this is because
the parser is a lot more complex in
Python 3.12 and because of that
complexity it can handle things like
nested F strings what is also of note is
that you get richer tracebacks when
something goes wrong inside of an
fstring because of the new added
functionality in the parser all right
everybody thank you so much for watching
hope to see you again in the next
one
